episode_plan_input.hook: Marco arrives at the old, abandoned house on Venn Street for his first day of cleaning work — alone, with only a broom and a bucket
key_events: Marco opens the tall cupboard in the back room to clean it and finds a heavy, dusty video camera sitting on the bottom shelf — therefore he picks it up and sees a tape already loaded inside it
He presses the power button but the screen stays dark and dead — but he notices a thin black cable coiled behind the camera, so he plugs it into the wall socket and the screen flickers on

The tape plays: it shows the same back room fifty years ago, bright and full of furniture, and a man and a woman kneel in the corner and push a small metal box under a loose floorboard — DRAMATIC IRONY: Marco is standing directly beside that corner the whole time he watches, without knowing it

Marco crosses to the corner, presses his foot on the boards one by one, and finds one that moves — it is loose, exactly as on the tape — but at that moment he hears the heavy front door of the house scrape open downstairs
episode_plan_input.ending_hook: Someone has just walked into the house below Marco — but his employer did not say anyone else was coming today, so who is it, and why are they here?

output:
<thinking>
Let me plan this episode carefully.

**1. Map key events to scenes:**

- **Opening/Hook (1-2 paragraphs):** Marco arrives at the abandoned house on Venn Street. Sets the atmosphere. He enters with broom and bucket.
- **Scene 1 (2-3 paragraphs):** Marco opens the tall cupboard in the back room. Finds the dusty video camera with a tape inside. He presses power — dark screen. Finds the cable, plugs it in, screen flickers on.
- **Scene 2 (2-3 paragraphs):** Tape plays — the same back room fifty years ago, bright, full of furniture. A man and woman kneel in the corner, push a small metal box under a loose floorboard. DRAMATIC IRONY: Marco is standing right beside that corner.
- **Scene 3 (1-2 paragraphs):** Marco crosses to the corner, tests the floorboards one by one with his foot, finds the loose one. Then hears the front door scrape open downstairs.
- **Ending hook:** Last line.

**2. Dialogue:**
The episode plan doesn't include other characters present with Marco. He's alone. So dialogue will be limited or absent unless I introduce something... The constraints say 40-50% dialogue. But Marco is alone in the house.

I could have Marco talk to himself (internal monologue-style dialogue). Or perhaps there's a brief exchange when he first arrives — maybe a neighbour, or a brief phone call with his employer before he enters.

I could:
1. Have him arrive and exchange words with a neighbour briefly
2. Have him call his employer to check something
3. Have him mutter to himself

A brief phone call with his employer makes sense. His employer could call him, or he could call someone. This also sets up context. Let me add:
- A brief phone call with his employer (establishing he's alone, micro-conflict about the job)
- Marco talks briefly to himself (short lines in quotes)

**3. Vocabulary targets — two appearances each:**
- abandoned — (1) describing the house on arrival, (2) describing how the room looks/feels abandoned
- dusty — (1) the camera on the shelf, (2) the room/floor is dusty
- tape — (1) seeing the tape inside the camera, (2) when the tape plays
- floorboard — (1) man and woman push box under a floorboard on the tape, (2) Marco tests the floorboards
- loose — (1) on the tape: pushing under a loose floorboard, (2) Marco finds the loose board
- metal — (1) the small metal box on the tape, (2) the metal camera or cable
- cable — (1) Marco finds the thin black cable, (2) he plugs the cable in

**4. Dramatic irony:**
Marco is standing in that same corner while watching the tape. I'll describe him leaning against the wall near the corner, while the tape shows the exact same corner. The reader can see the connection; Marco cannot.

**5. Sentence length check:**
Average 8-9 words. Short sentences for tension at the end. No sentence over 15 words.
</thinking>

The house on Venn Street was abandoned.
Tall weeds grew by the front door.
Marco stood on the step with a broom and a bucket.
His phone buzzed. It was his employer, Mr. Osei.

"You are at the house?" Mr. Osei asked.
"Yes," Marco said. "But it is very big."
"Only the back room today. Clean it well."
"And I am alone? No one else is coming?"
"Just you. It is a simple job, Marco."
Marco looked at the dark windows. Simple was not the word he thought of.

Inside, the air was cold and dusty.
Every step left a mark on the floor.
Marco went to the back room and opened the tall cupboard.
The smell was old and strange.

On the bottom shelf sat a video camera.
It was dusty and very heavy.
Marco picked it up and turned it over in his hands.
A tape was already inside it.
"Who left this here?" Marco said.
He pressed the power button. The screen was dark and dead.

Marco put the camera on the floor.
Then he saw something behind it — a thin black cable.
He pulled the cable out slowly.
One end of the cable went into the camera.
The other end went into the wall socket.
The screen flickered. Then it came on.

The tape began to play.
The image was old and yellow.
But Marco could see the back room.
This same room — fifty years ago.

The room on the tape was bright and full of furniture.
Chairs and boxes and a large table filled the space.
Then two people came into the image.
A man and a woman.
They carried a small metal box between them.
They moved quickly, like people in a hurry.
They walked to the corner of the room.

Marco watched the screen.
He was standing in that same corner right now.
He did not look down at his own feet.

On the tape, the man knelt down.
He pulled at a floorboard with his fingers.
The floorboard was loose.
The woman pushed the metal box into the dark space below.
The man put the floorboard back.
Then they both left the room.
The tape went dark.

Marco looked at the corner around his feet.
He pressed his foot on one board.
It was hard and solid.
He tried the next one.
Then the next.

His foot pressed down on a board near the wall.
It moved.
The floorboard was loose, just like on the tape.

[OUTPUT TRUNCATED — hit max_tokens limit of 2000]

observations.dialogue_handling: Writer creatively solved the solo-character dialogue problem by inventing a phone call with the employer (Mr. Osei) and having Marco talk to himself. Not in the episode plan but consistent with it. Micro-conflict present: employer calls it 'a simple job', Marco disagrees internally.
observations.dramatic_irony: Well executed — Marco standing in the corner while watching the tape show the same corner. Reader sees the connection, Marco does not.
observations.cut_off: Output was cut mid-sentence — Writer ran out of tokens. max_tokens was 2000, needs to be increased to 3000.
observations.thinking_block: Useful to keep during development. Model self-checked word counts and planned dialogue placement. Not wasted — helps catch constraint violations before prose is written.
observations.story_bible_not_saved: Director output contains both story_bible and episode_plan but only episode_plan was extracted and passed to Writer. Story bible is currently discarded. Next step: save story_bible to file for Episode 2 continuity.

Print story_bible so it is visible before Writer runs
